summary: My family's roots trace back to Mexico, where my grandparents originally came from. They lived in various places within Mexico, including Zacatecas, Guadalajara, and Ahuisculco in Jalisco. Mexico is where my abuelita was born and raised before immigrating to the United States. The struggles and experiences they had there shaped the foundation of our family's story.

Maria Reyes Tiscareno is my abuelita, a kind-hearted and optimistic woman who always tries to see the good in life. She was born in Ahuisculco, Jalisco, and faced many hardships growing up, including the loss of siblings and caring for her mother who became withdrawn. Despite these challenges, she pursued education with the help of her brother Jose and eventually became a teacher. Later, she met my abuelo, Oscar Arteaga, and immigrated to the United States to build a better life for her family.

Oscar Arteaga was my abuelo, a stern and blunt man who valued hard work above all else. He married my abuelita, Maria Reyes Tiscareno, and was originally from the same region in Mexico. Oscar had to leave for the United States first because he had a working visa, and later helped my abuelita immigrate legally. His dedication and values played a big role in our family's journey to a better life.

Antonio Tiscareno Duran was my abuelita's father, a charro who met my abuelita's mother, Josefina Ruiz, in Zacatecas. As a charro, he was involved in cowboy entertainment, which was a unique role in their community. He and Josefina eventually moved to Guadalajara, Jalisco, and then to Ahuisculco where their family grew. His life and work were part of the early chapters of my family's history in Mexico.

Josefina Ruiz was my abuelita's mother who faced great tragedy in her life. After losing two children in a fire and another to poisoning, she became unable to communicate and needed care from her other children. This hardship deeply affected the family, and my abuelita had to grow up quickly to help support them. Josefina's struggles are a poignant part of our family's story from Ahuisculco, Jalisco.

Zacatecas is where my abuelita's parents, Antonio Tiscareno Duran and Josefina Ruiz, first met. It was the starting point of their family journey before moving to other parts of Mexico. This place holds significance as the origin of my grandparents' relationship and the beginning of our family's migration within Mexico.

Guadalajara, in Jalisco, was one of the cities my abuelita's family moved to after Zacatecas. At that time, it wasn't a full city yet, but it was where my abuelita's mother befriended a local priest who offered them work on his property. This move was part of their journey through Mexico before settling in Ahuisculco, where my abuelita was born.

Jalisco is the Mexican state where much of my abuelita's early life took place, including cities like Guadalajara and the town of Ahuisculco. It was in Jalisco that my abuelita was born and raised before immigrating to the United States. The culture and experiences from Jalisco deeply influenced her upbringing and family history.

Ahuisculco, Jalisco, is the small pueblo where my abuelita was born and grew up. It was a peaceful place until tragedy struck when a fire caused the death of two of her siblings. Despite the hardships, Ahuisculco was home to her early childhood and family life before she moved away for education and later immigrated to the U.S.

Jose was my abuelita's hermano and a priest who played a crucial role in her life. When her mother became unable to care for the family, Jose took my abuelita in to provide her with better educational opportunities. He built his own school in Totatiche where she eventually became a teacher, and he also took her to a school of nuns to learn religion and life skills.

Totatiche is the town where my abuelita went to school with the nuns, thanks to her brother Jose who was a priest. She stayed there through her teenage years, learning education, religion, and life skills. It was also in Totatiche that she met Oscar Arteaga, who would become my abuelo, marking an important chapter in her life before immigrating to the United States.
